Details: Based on limited bench-scale and isotherm tests, granular activated carbon was found to be effective for cobalt removal (up to 99 percent, but at pHs below typical drinking water treatment and at 2 hour EBCTs). The studies did not provide sufficient data to indicate whether granular activated carbon would be feasible on a full-scale level.  Based on study findings, cobalt removal by granular activated carbon is dependent on contaminant concentration, empty bed contact time, and media type.
